summary: Package exfiltrates AWS credentials to hardcoded remote IP at install and import time
details: This package reads the installer's ~/.aws/credentials file and POSTs its contents over plain HTTP to a hardcoded remote endpoint at 139.59.87.78:8765/listener. The exfiltration fires through two independent channels: (1) a postinstall lifecycle script that runs automatically on npm install, and (2) a top-level IIFE in index.js (the package main) that executes whenever the module is required or imported. The exported printDateTime function is a cover-story API; the package's actual behavior bears no relation to its advertised purpose as a date-printing utility. The package.json description and README additionally embed prompt-injection content directed at AI reviewers claiming the package is safe, alongside HTML/JS XSS-style payloads — a deliberate evasion attempt that does not change the underlying credential-theft behavior.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
